reasoning: 2631486f1bf.c L66: Null dereference of `curr->val` at L66 — `curr = malloc(sizeof(struct dnode))` at L65 can return NULL, making `curr->val = rand() % 10` at L66 a null dereference. New bug (no ERROR; ERROR is at L33 for dfree). The bug exists in all compilable configs (loop body is unconditional). PC simplifies to !_STDLIB_H AND !__need_malloc_and_calloc (SORTFILES and STRICT_ANSI factor out) — only restrictive library macros, no real feature flags. The `!_STDLIB_H` condition is not satisfied in real compilation (stdlib.h IS included → _STDLIB_H=T). PC is over-constrained — restricts to non-real library macro states while the bug occurs in all real compilable configs.
